draft.introduction: The second Servant Song, in which the servant speaks in the first person about his mission to restore Israel and become “a light for the nations.” Paul quotes this passage to justify the Gentile mission (Acts 13:47).

translation.text: Listen to me, O coastlands, and give attention, you peoples from afar. The Lord called me from the womb; from the body of my mother he named my name. He said to me, “You are my servant, Israel, in whom I will be glorified.” I will make you as a light for the nations, that my salvation may reach to the end of the earth.
translation.original_text: ἀκούσατέ μου νῆσοι καὶ προσέχετε ἔθνη

translator_notes.note: “A light for the nations” (φῶς ἐθνῶν) is echoed in Simeon’s canticle (Luke 2:32) and Paul’s self-defense (Acts 13:47). The universalism of this passage was decisive for early Christian theology of Gentile inclusion.
